title: Highest temperature in Seoul on July 19?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 19 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Monsoon-season dynamics and short-range variability primarily shape trader positioning for Seoul’s July 19 high temperature.** Persistent Changma front activity brings high humidity (often 80–90 %), frequent cloud cover, and scattered showers that limit daytime solar heating and cap peak readings, favoring the closely clustered 27–28 °C outcomes (combined >50 % implied probability). Recent July settlements under similar conditions—28 °C on July 15 and 27 °C on July 9—illustrate how rain and moisture consistently suppress maxima below drier-scenario levels.

Model consensus points to modest day-to-day fluctuations driven by the exact timing and intensity of any showers or breaks in cloud cover, which can shift the daily maximum by 1–3 °C. Official guidance from the Korea Meteorological Administration and global ensembles shows typical mid-July highs near 28–30 °C, with the upper tail (30 °C or 31 °C+) requiring clearer skies and stronger subsidence, while cooler outcomes (≤26 °C) would need heavier or more prolonged precipitation. With resolution only two days away, traders closely monitor the latest KMA updates and mesoscale model runs for any shifts in the monsoon boundary or rainfall timing that would tip the balance among the tightly bunched leading contracts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Short-term numerical weather prediction models from MetService and international sources currently indicate a daytime maximum near 14°C for Wellington on July 19 under light easterly to southeasterly flow and partly cloudy conditions. This aligns with the market’s strong consensus on 14°C (53%) and 13°C (31%), reflecting typical mid-winter variability where small shifts in frontal timing, wind strength, or insolation can alter the peak by 1°C. July climatology shows average highs of 12–13°C, and NIWA’s seasonal outlook assigns roughly equal chances of near-average or below-average temperatures through the period. Recent days, including a confirmed 15°C high on July 14, demonstrate how transient northerly influences can briefly elevate readings above the mean, keeping traders attentive to the next model runs and official MetService updates ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Japan Meteorological Agency short-range forecasts and ensemble model runs for July 19 point to a Tokyo maximum near 31–32°C under the influence of an extended Pacific subtropical high, explaining the near-tie between those two outcomes at roughly 32% and 31.5% implied probability. Persistent high pressure favors clear to partly cloudy conditions and subsidence warming, yet modest differences in afternoon cloud cover, sea-breeze timing, or urban heat-island intensity—amplified by Tokyo’s dense built environment—can shift the peak by 1°C. Seasonal outlooks note above-normal baseline temperatures from global warming and a northward-shifted westerly jet, but short-term variability in moisture and localized convection keeps lower (30°C) and higher (33°C+) outcomes viable until updated guidance narrows the range.

eventMetadata.context_description: Singapore’s Meteorological Service Singapore tracks daily maxima near the 31–33°C climatological July range under prevailing Southwest Monsoon flow, with southeasterly winds and maritime moderation keeping extremes rare. Recent subseasonal guidance highlights warmer-than-average conditions across the western Maritime Continent alongside continued afternoon thundery showers that can suppress peaks through evaporative cooling and cloud cover. Model consensus therefore centers probabilities on 32°C and 33°C, with smaller shares for 31°C reflecting uncertainty in shower timing and intensity over the next 48 hours. El Niño development adds a modest upward bias to baseline temperatures but does not override local convective variability that traders weigh against official forecast updates.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Trader sentiment for Taipei’s July 19 maximum temperature reflects typical mid-July subtropical variability, with market-implied probabilities clustered tightly around 35–37 °C.** Official guidance from Taiwan’s Central Weather Administration (CWA) and ensemble model runs indicate a post-typhoon environment featuring moderate subsidence, partly cloudy skies, and a modest chance of afternoon convective showers that can limit peak heating. Historical climatology places average daily maxima near 33–34 °C, but recent days have reached 35–36 °C under similar clear-to-partly-cloudy regimes.

Key differentiating factors include the precise timing and coverage of diurnal thunderstorms, boundary-layer moisture, and urban heat-island amplification in the Taipei Basin. Stronger insolation and lighter winds favor readings near or above 36 °C, while earlier or more widespread convection caps the maximum closer to 35 °C. Current forecasts from multiple sources converge on 33–35 °C, yet the 26.5 % probability on 37 °C captures the realistic upside if subsidence strengthens and showers remain suppressed. With resolution only two days away, updates from the CWA’s 7-day outlook and high-resolution model guidance will be the primary drivers shifting these closely matched probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Trader sentiment for Busan's July 19 maximum temperature clusters tightly around 31–32°C because short-range forecast models show genuine uncertainty in afternoon conditions amid typical mid-July East Asian monsoon influences.** Ensemble guidance from agencies such as the Korea Meteorological Administration places the most likely outcomes near these thresholds, consistent with climatological July highs of 29–31°C moderated by sea breezes off the Korea Strait. 

Elevated humidity, variable cloud cover, and the potential for scattered showers or thundershowers can suppress peak heating by limiting solar insolation, while any earlier clearing or reduced precipitation under the influence of the North Pacific High would allow temperatures to edge toward 32–33°C. Historical analogs and current model spreads highlight how small differences in steering patterns, boundary-layer moisture, and localized coastal effects create the observed market split, with no single dominant driver yet resolved in the latest runs. Updated model guidance and official briefings over the next 48 hours remain the primary near-term catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus around 32–33°C for Kuala Lumpur’s July 19 high reflects ensemble model guidance from ECMWF and GFS runs showing afternoon maxima near the long-term July average of 32.5°C. Persistent low-level moisture, typical of the southwest monsoon, supports scattered convection that often caps daytime heating through cloud cover and brief showers, keeping outcomes clustered in this narrow band. Recent upper-level ridging has been weak, limiting extreme heat potential, while sea-surface temperatures in the Strait of Malacca remain near normal and provide little additional thermal boost. Model spread on timing and intensity of afternoon thunderstorms creates the observed 41.5 %–33.5 % split between the two leading bins, with later runs expected to narrow uncertainty ahead of the 19th.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Recent forecasts for Qingdao on July 19, 2026, center on a daily high near 29–30°C under mostly cloudy skies, aligning closely with the market’s top outcomes at 26.5% each for 29°C and 30°C.** Coastal sea-breeze circulation off the Yellow Sea typically caps afternoon heating by advecting cooler marine air onshore, while summer monsoon moisture supports scattered cloud cover and light showers that reduce surface insolation and limit temperature spikes. Ensemble guidance from agencies like the China Meteorological Administration reflects modest spread from variable cloud thickness and precipitation timing, which can shift the maximum by 1–2°C and keeps adjacent brackets (28°C at 19.5%, 31°C at 21.5%) tightly competitive.

Historical July climatology shows average highs of 27–29°C, so current conditions represent a modest positive anomaly driven by regional warmth, yet the coastal setting and potential for marine-layer influence prevent extremes above 32°C from gaining traction. Traders appear to weigh the balance between sufficient daytime heating under partly cloudy skies and moderation from onshore flow or brief rain, producing the observed clustering around the 28–31°C range ahead of updated 48-hour model runs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent model consensus from Météo-France and ECMWF points to a moderating high-pressure regime over northern France, with surface highs settling near 25–26°C in central Paris on July 19 after the early-July heat dome. Key differentiators include variable low-level moisture, potential afternoon cumulus development that caps insolation, and the strength of the urban heat-island effect under light winds. Historical July maxima average 25–27°C, so traders weigh how slight shifts in 500-hPa heights or boundary-layer mixing could nudge the peak across the tightly bunched 24–26°C brackets. Updated high-resolution runs and Météo-France’s next bulletin will likely refine these probabilities before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Latest official and model guidance for Warsaw on July 19, 2026, centers on a daily maximum of 25–27°C, with the market-implied probabilities (27°C at 35.5%, 26°C at 27%) reflecting tight ensemble spread in global numerical weather prediction systems such as ECMWF and GFS.**

Forecasts from sources including the UK Met Office (26°C) and BBC Weather (25°C) align with this range, while longer-term climatology places the July average high near 24–25°C. Recent developments include a late-June European heatwave that set Polish records above 40°C, followed by cooler early-July conditions; the current setup shows a moderating high-pressure influence with limited cloud cover and southerly flow, supporting modest warming above baseline without extreme advection.

Differentiation between the closely matched leading outcomes hinges on small variations in key physical processes: surface solar insolation under partly clear skies, the precise timing and strength of any weak frontal passage that could increase low-level moisture or reduce daytime heating, boundary-layer mixing depth, and minor differences in 850 hPa temperatures across model members. Ensembles capture this uncertainty, keeping 26°C and 27°C probabilities highest while assigning low odds to outliers above 29°C or below 24°C. Updated model runs and official briefings in the next 24–48 hours will likely narrow the range ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Current forecasts for Jeddah on July 19 center on a daily maximum near 39–41 °C, producing the tight clustering of market-implied odds around the 39 °C, 40 °C, and 41 °C-or-higher brackets.** Numerical weather prediction ensembles show modest spread driven by coastal Red Sea humidity, which typically limits afternoon peaks a few degrees below inland values, combined with prevailing northwesterly flow that enhances mixing and sea-breeze moderation. July climatology places the long-term mean daily maximum near 37–39 °C, with occasional excursions above 40 °C when regional subsidence strengthens under stable high pressure. Traders are weighing this baseline against short-range model updates that could shift the exact peak by 1–2 °C depending on precise timing of maximum insolation, boundary-layer moisture, and any subtle changes in steering winds. With resolution hinging on the official Saudi National Center for Meteorology observation, the close probabilities reflect genuine uncertainty in the final 1–2 °C of the forecast envelope rather than divergent scientific signals.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Recent forecasts from numerical weather models and the Servicio Meteorológico Nacional indicate a daily maximum near 13°C for Buenos Aires on July 19, consistent with the market’s leading outcomes clustered at 13–15°C (combined ~80% implied probability).** Mid-July places the region in the heart of Southern Hemisphere winter, when climatological average highs hover around 14–15°C under the influence of cooler maritime polar air masses and frequent frontal passages. A warming spell earlier in the week, driven by northwesterly flow that pushed temperatures toward 22–24°C on July 17, has given way to a southward surge of cooler air, lowering expected maxima and tightening the distribution around 13–15°C. Ensemble guidance shows limited spread, with resolution hinging on official station readings that capture urban heat-island effects and precise timing of any residual cloud cover or light precipitation. Traders are pricing in this post-frontal cooling while assigning low probability to extremes, reflecting both the narrow forecast range and historical July variability rarely exceeding 18–19°C or dropping below 11°C on any single day.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Current short-range numerical weather prediction models, including those from Environment Canada and major global ensembles, indicate a highest temperature near 25 °C for Toronto on July 19, supported by a ridge of high pressure promoting mostly sunny conditions with light northwesterly flow and modest surface heating.** This places the market-implied probability peak at 25 °C (36 %) and 24 °C (28.5 %), reflecting tight clustering around typical mid-July climatology of ~26 °C while accounting for model spread in boundary-layer mixing and any residual smoke or humidity effects. Differentiation among 24–26 °C outcomes stems from subtle variations in forecasted afternoon dew points, wind speeds, and the precise timing of any weak trough passage, with lower probabilities assigned to 27 °C+ (under 7 %) or cooler readings due to limited support for stronger advection or cloud cover in the latest runs. Traders monitor the next model cycles and official Environment Canada updates for any shifts ahead of the daily maximum observation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service and model guidance for Seattle-Tacoma International Airport points to a daily high near 78°F on July 19, reflecting moderate onshore flow from the Pacific and a stable marine layer that limits daytime warming in the Puget Sound region. eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for central Texas indicate a highest temperature in the low-to-mid 90s°F for July 19, 2026, driven by typical midsummer southerly flow, abundant moisture, and partial cloud cover that limits full insolation. No strong high-pressure ridge or heat-advisory conditions are expected to push readings into the upper 90s or above, keeping 96°F+ outcomes at combined probabilities below 30%. Updated NWS and NOAA model runs ahead of the date remain the key near-term catalyst for any shifts in trader positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service show San Francisco’s July 19 high likely settling in the mid-to-upper 60s as a persistent marine layer moderates onshore flow and limits daytime heating. The closely matched market probabilities around 64–69 °F reflect small differences among ensemble runs in marine-layer depth, low-cloud clearance timing, and local sea-breeze strength. Cooler sea-surface temperatures along the central coast reinforce the onshore gradient, while any weakening of the subtropical high could allow slightly warmer air to reach the city. Traders are therefore weighting the most probable range near the climatological July average, with only modest upside risk if the layer erodes earlier than expected.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models from sources like AccuWeather and regional analyses indicate a daily maximum temperature near 25°C for Mexico City on July 19, aligning with the market's leading 38.5% implied probability for that outcome and 27.5% for 26°C. Recent model runs show no significant deviation from these norms, with light precipitation expected to cap extremes, supporting the tight clustering around 24–26°C while assigning low odds to outliers above 27°C or below 23°C. Updated guidance closer to resolution will refine these trader-consensus probabilities.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Marcos A. Gelabert Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pa/panama-city/MPMG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Short-term forecast uncertainty around cloud cover, rainfall timing, and daytime heating in Panama City's tropical wet-season environment is the main driver keeping market-implied odds clustered near 31–33 °C. Typical July highs average 31–32 °C, but afternoon convective storms often limit peak temperatures while variable humidity and sea-breeze effects can push readings slightly higher on clearer days. Official guidance from regional models shows close agreement on the central tendency yet modest spread from steering patterns and localized precipitation, producing the observed tight distribution across adjacent outcomes. Final station observations on July 19 will resolve the market.
